steps.title: Set up the equation
steps.content:
We need the number of games when total cost is \$58. Substitute into the function:
$$14 + 4x = 58$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
Subtract 14 from both sides:
$$4x = 44$$
Divide both sides by 4:
$$x = 11$$
Therefore, 11 games can be played for \$58.
